title: Apple Intelligence Isn't Driving iPhone Upgrades
content:
Apple Intelligence hasn't convinced people to buy an iPhone 16, Apple analyst Ming-Chi Kuo said today. According to Kuo, a supply chain survey suggests ‚ÄåApple Intelligence‚Äå is not pushing people to upgrade their devices.


The delay between the ‚ÄåApple Intelligence‚Äå introduction in June 2024 and a launch even after ‚ÄåiPhone 16‚Äå models came out was a contributing factor, and ‚ÄåApple Intelligence‚Äå "appeal has significantly declined" compared to cloud-based services like ChatGPT.

There is also no indication that ‚ÄåApple Intelligence‚Äå will drive Services revenue, as Apple CEO Tim Cook said in December that Apple has never discussed charging for ‚ÄåApple Intelligence‚Äå. Bloomberg's Mark Gurman said that Apple could start charging for ‚ÄåApple Intelligence‚Äå in 2027 or beyond, but it remains to be seen if that will happen.

All in all, Kuo does not feel that ‚ÄåApple Intelligence‚Äå is going to benefit Apple in terms of user upgrades or revenue, but there's some hope for the long-term prospects.
I'm not bearish on Apple Intelligence's long-term prospects; however, given the points discussed above, there is no evidence of Apple Intelligence's ability to benefit hardware replacement cycles or service business. As such, it should be cautious of potential downside risks created by earlier market over-optimism.

‚ÄåApple Intelligence‚Äå was the main focus of iOS 18, but when the ‚ÄåiPhone 16‚Äå models launched in September, there were no ‚ÄåApple Intelligence‚Äå features available. Writing Tools and other capabilities came in the first update to ‚ÄåiOS 18‚Äå in October, but Image Playground and Genmoji didn't roll out until December.

There are still ‚ÄåApple Intelligence‚Äå features that have not been implemented, including major changes to Siri that are rumored to be coming in April.

A December survey suggested that many iPhone users feel that ‚ÄåApple Intelligence‚Äå has added little or no value to their ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå experience, though that sentiment could change as ‚ÄåApple Intelligence‚Äå matures.
